---
title: "NNETAR fDOM Forecasting — Rolling-Window Daily Refit"
output:
  html_document:
    toc: true
    toc_float: true
    toc_depth: 2
    theme: flatly
date: "`r Sys.Date()`"
---


## Packages and Data

Like `3c_Predict_NNETAR_dailyRefit.Rmd`, a fresh NNETAR is trained at every reference
date (both AR structure and network weights are re-estimated). The difference is the
training window: instead of an **expanding** window (all history back to the start
of the record), each refit here uses a **rolling** window of only the `window_years`
immediately preceding the reference date. `get_train_window()` defines that window
once, and the same function is used both for the initial illustrative fit (relative
to `train_cutoff`) and for every refit inside the forecast loop (relative to each
`forecast_date`).

No `lag_col` argument is needed — NNETAR selects its autoregressive order `p`
automatically. `n_networks = 20` averages over 20 random initialisations for stability
(Melofton et al. approach).

```{r packages}
library(tidyverse)
library(lubridate)
library(fable)
library(fabletools)
library(tsibble)
library(zoo)        # na.approx for short-gap interpolation
```

```{r load-data}
getwd()
ar_df <- read_csv("../Data/Daily_catwalk_RH_2021_2026.csv") |>
  mutate(Date = as.Date(Date)) |>
  select(-fDOM_1m_lag1)   # lag column not needed — NNETAR handles AR lags internally

# Training window: all data before 2024
train_cutoff <- ymd("2024-01-01")

# Prediction window: every day of 2024 and 2025
pred_dates <- seq(ymd("2024-01-01"), ymd("2025-12-31"), by = "day")
```


```{r interpolate-short-gaps}
# Interpolate short gaps only (≤ 10 consecutive missing days) in target and all drivers.
# Gaps longer than 10 days are left as NA — those reference dates will be skipped
ar_df <- ar_df |>
  mutate(across(-Date,
                \(x) zoo::na.approx(x, maxgap = 10, na.rm = FALSE, rule = 2)))

```

```{r fn-window}
# Rolling training window: only the `window_years` immediately preceding
# `reference_date`. Used for both the initial model fit (reference_date =
# train_cutoff) and every daily refit in the forecast loop (reference_date =
# forecast_date), so the same window logic is applied consistently everywhere
# training data gets filtered.
get_train_window <- function(reference_date, window_years = 2) {
  list(
    # %m-% (not plain -) so leap-day reference dates roll back to the last
    # valid day of the target month instead of returning NA (e.g. 2024-02-29
    # %m-% years(2) -> 2022-02-28, rather than NA because 2022-02-29 doesn't exist)
    start = reference_date %m-% years(window_years),
    end   = reference_date
  )
}

# Single place to change the rolling window length used everywhere below
window_years <- 2

```

---

## Model Fitting

### Fitting function

Fits a NNETAR model via `fable::NNETAR()` on the rolling window of data ending at
`train_cutoff`. `n_networks = 20` averages over 20 random network initialisations
for stability (Melofton et al. approach). Autoregressive order `p` and hidden node
count `k` are selected automatically.

`report()` is called after fitting to print the selected NNAR(p,k) structure.

Returns the fitted mable, training plot, and metadata needed for the forecast function.

```{r fn-fit}
fit_nnetar_model <- function(all_data,
                              train_cutoff,
                              target,
                              covariates   = NULL,
                              model_name,
                              n_networks   = 20,
                              window_years = 2,
                              seed         = 100) {

  set.seed(seed)

  col_select <- c(target, covariates)

  train_window <- get_train_window(train_cutoff, window_years)

  train_df <- all_data |>
    filter(Date >= train_window$start, Date < train_window$end) |>
    select(Date, all_of(col_select)) |>
    arrange(Date)

  train_ts <- as_tsibble(train_df, index = Date)

  message(model_name, " | n_train = ", nrow(train_df))

  # Fit NNETAR — univariate or with external regressors
  if (is.null(covariates)) {
    nnetar_fit <- train_ts |>
      model(nnetar = NNETAR(!!sym(target), n_networks = n_networks))
  } else {
    formula_obj <- as.formula(paste(target, "~",
                                    paste(covariates, collapse = " + ")))
    nnetar_fit <- train_ts |>
      model(nnetar = NNETAR(formula = formula_obj, n_networks = n_networks))
  }

  # Print selected NNAR(p,k) structure
  message(model_name, " | model structure:")
  report(nnetar_fit)

  # In-sample fitted values
  fitted_vals <- fitted(nnetar_fit) |>
    as_tibble() |>
    select(Date, .fitted)

  train_df_plot <- train_df |>
    left_join(fitted_vals, by = "Date")

  train_rmse <- sqrt(mean((train_df_plot[[target]] - train_df_plot$.fitted)^2,
                           na.rm = TRUE))

  # Training plot: observed vs fitted timeseries
  p_fit <- ggplot(train_df_plot) +
    geom_point(aes(x = Date, y = !!sym(target), color = "Observed"),
               size = 1.2, alpha = 0.8) +
    geom_point(aes(x = Date, y = .fitted, color = "NNETAR fitted"),
               size = 1.2, alpha = 0.8) +
    scale_color_manual(values = c("Observed" = "black", "NNETAR fitted" = "dodgerblue"),
                       breaks = c("Observed", "NNETAR fitted")) +
    labs(title = paste0(model_name, " — training fit (", window_years, "yr window ending ",
                        train_window$end, ")",
                        "  |  RMSE = ", round(train_rmse, 2), " QSU"),
         x = "Date", y = "fDOM (QSU)", color = NULL) +
    theme_bw() +
    theme(legend.position = "top")

  list(
    model        = nnetar_fit,
    plot         = p_fit,
    model_name   = model_name,
    target       = target,
    covariates   = covariates,
    n_networks   = n_networks,
    window_years = window_years,
    train_rmse   = train_rmse
  )
}
```

### Fit 4 models

Initial fit on the `r window_years`-year rolling window ending at `r train_cutoff` —
used for plot inspection and NNAR(p,k) structure review. A fresh model is trained at
every reference date in the forecast loop below.

```{r fit-models}
# NNETAR1: univariate (AR lags of fDOM only)
# NNETAR_lagonly <- fit_nnetar_model(
#   all_data     = ar_df,
#   train_cutoff = train_cutoff,
#   target       = "fDOM_1_QSU_daily",
#   covariates   = NULL,
#   model_name   = "NNETAR_lag_only",
#   window_years = window_years
# )

# NNETAR2: + in-lake (Temp, DO, Chla)
NNETAR_lake <- fit_nnetar_model(
  all_data     = ar_df,
  train_cutoff = train_cutoff,
  target       = "fDOM_1_QSU_daily",
  covariates   = c("Diff_Dens_1_max", "DOsat_1_pct_daily", "Chla_1_ugL_daily"),
  model_name   = "NNETAR_lag_lake",
  window_years = window_years
)

# NNETAR3: + catchment (RH discharge and DOC)
NNETAR_catch <- fit_nnetar_model(
  all_data     = ar_df,
  train_cutoff = train_cutoff,
  target       = "fDOM_1_QSU_daily",
  covariates   = c("RH_Q_cms", "RH_DOC_mgL"),
  model_name   = "NNETAR_lag_catchment",
  window_years = window_years
)

# NNETAR4: + in-lake + catchment
NNETAR_lake_catch <- fit_nnetar_model(
  all_data     = ar_df,
  train_cutoff = train_cutoff,
  target       = "fDOM_1_QSU_daily",
  covariates   = c("Diff_Dens_1_max", "DOsat_1_pct_daily", "Chla_1_ugL_daily",
                   "RH_Q_cms", "RH_DOC_mgL"),
  model_name   = "NNETAR_lag_lake_catchment",
  window_years = window_years
)

```

### Training plots

```{r plot-training, fig.height=4}
NNETAR_lake$plot
NNETAR_catch$plot
NNETAR_lake_catch$plot
```

---

## 30-Day Forecast Function

A fresh NNETAR is trained at every reference date, but only on the rolling
`window_years` window ending at that date (via `get_train_window()`) — not the full
expanding history. `forecast()` then projects 30 days forward from the freshly
trained model.

For covariate models, observed covariate values for the 30 forecast days are supplied
via `new_data` (perfect covariate assumption, same as all other scripts).

Note: refitting at each reference date is computationally intensive (`n_networks = 20`
neural nets per date per model). Expect several minutes per model.

```{r fn-forecast}
# # inputs to test function
# forecast_date <- ymd("2024-01-01")
# model_obj <- NNETAR_lake_catch
# all_data <- ar_df
# target  = "fDOM_1_QSU_daily"
# horizon = 30

forecast_fdom_30day <- function(forecast_date,
                                 model_obj,
                                 all_data,
                                 target       = "fDOM_1_QSU_daily",
                                 horizon      = 30,
                                 window_years = 2) {

  covariates <- model_obj$covariates
  n_networks <- model_obj$n_networks

  # Check observed fDOM exists on reference date
  curr_fdom <- all_data |>
    filter(Date == forecast_date) |>
    pull(!!sym(target))

  if (length(curr_fdom) == 0 || is.na(curr_fdom)) return(NULL)

  # Rolling window_years window up to and including the reference date
  train_window <- get_train_window(forecast_date, window_years)

  history_df <- all_data |>
    filter(Date >= train_window$start, Date <= train_window$end) |>
    select(Date, all_of(c(target, covariates))) |>
    arrange(Date)

  history_ts <- as_tsibble(history_df, index = Date)

  # Refit NNETAR on the rolling window (Melofton et al. approach — new model
  # per reference date, just bounded to the rolling window instead of all history)
  if (is.null(covariates)) {
    model_updated <- history_ts |>
      model(nnetar = NNETAR(!!sym(target), n_networks = n_networks))
  } else {
    formula_obj <- as.formula(paste(target, "~",
                                    paste(covariates, collapse = " + ")))
    model_updated <- history_ts |>
      model(nnetar = NNETAR(formula = formula_obj, n_networks = n_networks))
  }

  # Forecast dates: day 1 through day 30 after reference date
  forecast_dates <- seq(forecast_date + 1, forecast_date + horizon, by = "day")

  # Generate forecast; this sets up future timeseries with drivers; don't need the lagged observations since thats stored in the model fit
  if (!is.null(covariates)) {
    future_ts <- all_data |>
      filter(Date %in% forecast_dates) |>
      select(Date, all_of(covariates)) |>
      arrange(Date) |>
      as_tsibble(index = Date)

    if (nrow(future_ts) == 0) return(NULL)

    #notes times = 0 specification is critical to avoid super slow runtimes, default is 5000
    #had used 31 for Howard et al. 2026 JGR since doing 4cast w/ uncertainty
    fc <- forecast(model_updated, new_data = future_ts, times = 0)
  } else {
    fc <- forecast(model_updated, h = horizon, times = 0)
  }

  fc |>
    as_tibble() |>
    select(Date, .mean) |>
    mutate(
      model_id          = model_obj$model_name,
      reference_date    = forecast_date,
      Horizon           = as.integer(Date - forecast_date),
      fDOM_1m_predicted = .mean
    ) |>
    select(model_id, reference_date, forecast_date = Date, Horizon, fDOM_1m_predicted) |>
    filter(Horizon > 0)
}

# # test function
# zz <- forecast_fdom_30day(forecast_date = ymd("2024-06-01"), model_obj = NNETAR_lake_catch,
#                     all_data = ar_df, target = "fDOM_1_QSU_daily", horizon = 30)

```

---

## Run Forecasts Across 2024-2025

`window_years` is passed through so the rolling-window length used for every daily
refit can be changed in one place.

```{r fn-loop}
run_forecasts <- function(model_obj,
                           all_data,
                           pred_dates,
                           target       = "fDOM_1_QSU_daily",
                           horizon      = 30,
                           window_years = 2) {

  valid_dates <- all_data |>
    filter(Date %in% pred_dates, !is.na(!!sym(target))) |>
    pull(Date)

  message(model_obj$model_name, " | forecasting ", length(valid_dates),
          " reference dates (", window_years, "yr rolling refit)...")

  map_dfr(valid_dates, \(d) {
    message("  -> starting forecast for ", d)
    forecast_fdom_30day(
      forecast_date = d,
      model_obj     = model_obj,
      all_data      = all_data,
      target        = target,
      horizon       = horizon,
      window_years  = window_years
    )
  })
}
```

takes ~ 2 hours to run all forecasts 
```{r run-forecasts}
# forecasts_nnetar1 <- run_forecasts(NNETAR_lagonly,    ar_df, pred_dates, window_years = window_years)
forecasts_nnetar2 <- run_forecasts(NNETAR_lake,       ar_df, pred_dates, window_years = window_years)
forecasts_nnetar3 <- run_forecasts(NNETAR_catch,      ar_df, pred_dates, window_years = window_years)
forecasts_nnetar4 <- run_forecasts(NNETAR_lake_catch, ar_df, pred_dates, window_years = window_years)

all_NNETAR_forecasts <- bind_rows(forecasts_nnetar2,
                                   forecasts_nnetar3, forecasts_nnetar4)

glimpse(all_NNETAR_forecasts)
```

---

## Save Output

```{r save}
write_csv(all_NNETAR_forecasts,
          "../Data/NNETAR_forecasts_2024-2025_RollingRefit_24july26.csv")

```
